lyrics:
The dock is warm against my back
The lake is glass without a sound
And nothing moves and nothing breaks
And summer holds me to the ground

To be all
To be nothing
To be wide as the lake and sky
To be all

I close my eyes - the blue breaks through
The dock still warm against my spine
There is no place I'm moving to
There is no edge, there is no line

To be all
To be nothing
To be wide as the lake and sky
To be all

The lake is still the lake. The dock is wood
I sit up slowly. Nothing was a lie
The sun is on the boards exactly where
the sun has always been. I count my breath
I count it twice. The blue is only blue
I close my eyes again - and find a man
with closed eyes, sitting on a wooden dock
The blue is still inside me. Still the blue

I press my back to wood
I close my eyes again
I make my breathing slow
The blue will not break in

What if there is no door
What if there is no we
What if there is no more
What if there's only me

The dock is turning cold
And still my hands are hands
The lake is turning gold
And I am just a man

What if there is no door
What if there is no we
What if there is no more
What if there's only me

To be all
To be nothing
To be wide as the lake and sky
To be all

To be we
persona:
## 4Castle

The Future of Rock, The Power of Harmony

**4Castle **is a genre-defying, five-piece supergroup under Maricopa Records, built on a foundation of powerful vocal harmonies, intricate musicianship, and fearless innovation. Rooted in rock, but unbound by limitations, their music fuses elements of progressive rock, funk, Latin rhythms, blues, and folk, delivering a sound that is both technically complex and emotionally resonant.

### **A Band Like No Other**

Each member of **4Castle **brings a distinct voice-both musically and vocally-to the mix:
🎸 **Raven Cortez**(Lead Guitar, Vocals) – A fiery virtuoso with Latin and blues influences, weaving face-melting solos into every performance.
🎸 **Skylar “Sky” Monroe**(Rhythm Guitar, Vocals) – The poetic soul of the band, crafting melodic foundations and lyrically rich storytelling.
🎹 **Jonah Vega**(Keyboards, Vocals) – The sonic architect, layering psychedelic synths, Hammond organ swells, and cinematic orchestrations.
🎸 **Malcolm “Mack” Bishop**(Bass, Vocals) – The groove master, blending funk, jazz, and progressive rock into fluid, deep-pocket basslines.
🥁 **Eli “Fox” Foster **(Drums, Vocals) – The wild heartbeat, pushing the band forward with thunderous fills, polyrhythmic grooves, and explosive energy.

### **What Defines 4Castle?**

- **Multi-Layered Vocal Harmonies**– All five members **sing**, creating **rich, choral-like textures**that elevate every song.

- **Complex Arrangements & Bold Songwriting**– Each track is an **immersive journey**, balancing **technical mastery with raw emotion**.

- **Rhythmic Power & Innovation**– From **tight funk grooves to shifting time signatures**, their music **moves you-literally and figuratively**.

- **Lyrically Challenging Yet Accessible**– Their songs explore **deep themes of life, change, and resilience**, wrapped in **memorable hooks and melodies**.

### **4Castle’s Sound**

Imagine putting Emerson, Lake & Palmer, Heart, Led Zeppelin, Sly Stone, and Carlos Santana in a blender. The result? **4Castle**. Their music pays homage to the greats while pushing rock’s boundaries into the future.

### **Join the Journey**

With Maricopa Records, **4Castle **is set to redefine what a modern rock band can be. Expect genre-bending albums, electrifying performances, and music that resonates across generations.

🚀 Follow **4Castle **as they build their legacy-one song, one harmony, and one unforgettable moment at a time.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Cmxf7xuTHzf7UahMK2xJh)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/4castle/1801052673)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-rooks-in-a-dark-setting-1.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track has a polished and expansive 80s pop-rock texture, creating an atmosphere that is both dreamlike and anthemic. The energy builds from a serene, relaxed feel in the verses into a driving, confident groove for the choruses, propelled by a punchy, gated drum sound. The production is clean and wide, skillfully balancing a dense arrangement of synths, clean electric guitars, and prominent drums so that each element occupies its own space. A defining quality is the powerful dynamic lift into the chorus, where the layered vocals create a massive, uplifting hook that feels like soaring through an open sky.

review.review_text: The lyric at the center of this one does the heavy lifting: 'The lake is still the lake. The dock is wood / I sit up slowly. Nothing was a lie' is the kind of grounded, plain-spoken writing that earns its transcendence rather than demanding it. 4Castle build the song carefully in G minor at a mid-tempo 107 BPM, letting the verses breathe in near-stillness before the chorus opens up into something genuinely wide and communal, those stacked voices landing with the kind of lift you get from peak-era Fleetwood Mac or early Peter Gabriel. The final pivot from 'To be all' to 'To be we' is small and perfect.
anchors_used: quoted lyric: 'The lake is still the lake. The dock is wood / I sit up slowly. Nothing was a lie'
final lyric pivot: 'To be all' to 'To be we'
comparison: peak-era Fleetwood Mac, early Peter Gabriel
